data.title: Key facts: Shell boosts Brazil projects stake; wins Tupi, Atapu bids
data.description:
• Shell's Brazilian unit raised its stake in the Atapu and Mero offshore oil projects, increasing interest in Atapu to 16.917% and Mero to 20%, effective 2027.1
  • Shell is considering a deal with Galp to tackle future output challenges, including a bid for Galp's Namibian asset or a full acquisition.2
  • Shell, in partnership with Petrobras, won two offshore areas in Brazil's Tupi and Atapu fields, bidding 7.79 billion reais for Tupi and 1 billion for Atapu, exceeding minimum prices.3

data.title: Key facts: GSK Buyback Reaches 237M Shares; CDC Talks Impact Vaccines
data.description:
• On December 4, 2025, GSK plc bought 11,764,733 shares in its buyback program, raising total treasury shares to 237,229,577, which is 5.82% of voting rights.1
  • GSK, with Merck, makes hepatitis B vaccines. Recent CDC ACIP talks on vaccination schedules could impact GSK's vaccine supply and distribution.2

data.title: Key facts: BP buys 1.5M shares; negotiating Castrol sale; Duqm project cancelled
data.description:
• On December 4, 2025, BP bought 1,496,180 shares in a buyback program, increasing its treasury shares to 843,131,940 on the London Stock Exchange and Cboe (UK).1
  • BP is negotiating to sell its Castrol lubricants unit to Stonepeak for over $8 billion. The sale process began in February, but no final deal is in place yet.23
  • BP's Duqm green hydrogen project in Oman has been cancelled due to a mutual agreement, following a reassessment of market conditions, as confirmed by Hydrom's managing director.4

data.title: Key facts: Vodafone's Vodacom to acquire 20% of Safaricom; Spain launches PremiumFiber; Share buyback completed
data.description:
• Vodafone's Vodacom will buy an extra 20% stake in Safaricom, increasing ownership to 55%. The €1.36 billion deal includes 15% from Kenya's government and 5% from Vodafone.12
  • Vodafone Spain launched a fibre joint venture, PremiumFiber, with MasOrange and GIC, covering 12 million homes and serving 5 million customers, generating €1.4 billion for dividends and financing.3
  • On December 4, 2025, Vodafone Group Plc completed a share buyback with Merrill Lynch, acquiring shares as part of a program started on November 11, 2025.4

data.title: Key facts: Ryanair Declares €0.193 Dividend; Major Holdings Notification
data.description:
• Ryanair Holdings plc declared an interim dividend of €0.193 per ordinary share for FY26, with a record date of 16 January 2026 and a payment date scheduled for 25 February 2026.1
  • On December 4, 2025, Ryanair Holdings PLC announced a notification of major holdings in the company, in accordance with the standard form TR-1.2

data.title: Key facts: Rio Tinto boosts copper targets; shares rise; plans emissions cut
data.description:
• Rio Tinto raised its 2025 copper production targets during its recent Capital Markets Day and outlined a strategic plan aimed at generating up to $10 billion.1
  • Rio Tinto (RIO) has experienced a positive impact on its share price attributed to rising copper and iron ore prices, positioning it among the top performers in the ASX 200 over the past week.2
  • Rio Tinto is targeting a 50% reduction in emissions as part of its decarbonization strategy, with a revised capital expenditure estimate of $1 to $2 billion allocated for this initiative through 2030.3

data.title: Key facts: Glencore cuts production target; plans new smelter; reduces jobs
data.description:
• Glencore has reduced its production target for the upcoming year, raising concerns regarding potential global supply shortages in the copper market.1
  • Glencore will develop a new copper smelter in Antofagasta with Codelco, processing 1.5 million metric tonnes annually. Construction starts in 2030, with operations expected by 2032-2033.2
  • Glencore has announced a reduction of 1,000 jobs as part of a cost-cutting initiative aimed at streamlining operations and improving efficiency.3

data.title: Key facts: British American Tobacco buys back shares; plans ITC Hotels stake sale
data.description:
• On December 4, 2025, British American Tobacco bought back shares from Goldman Sachs, reducing total shares to 2,181,395,546. Executives also purchased shares on December 3.1
  • British American Tobacco plans to sell 7% to 15.3% of its ITC Hotels stake, valued at up to $776 million, to reduce debt by 2026. The sale has a floor price of ₹205.65.234

data.title: Key facts: AstraZeneca secures drug pricing deal; stock declines 1.3%; showcases at ASH
data.description:
• AstraZeneca secured a drug pricing deal in October 2025, aligning U.S. prices with the lowest rates in other developed nations, similar to Pfizer's agreement in September.1
  • AstraZeneca's stock experienced a decline of 1.3%, aligning with a broader downturn in the pharmaceutical sector, which saw an overall decrease of nearly 1%.2
  • AstraZeneca showcased its largest-ever presence at the American Society of Hematology (ASH) conference, highlighting advancements in hematology and cell therapy.3

data.title: American playwright Jeremy O. Harris arrested in Japan on alleged drug smuggling
data.description:
By Kantaro Komiya and Satoshi Sugiyama

American actor and playwright Jeremy O. Harris, known for the Tony-nominated "Slave Play", was arrested last month at an airport in Japan on suspicion of attempting to smuggle illegal drugs into the country, local authorities said late on Thursday.

Harris, 36, was stopped on November 16 at Naha Airport on Okinawa island after a customs officer discovered 0.78 grams of crystal containing the synthetic narcotic MDMA in his tote bag, an Okinawa Regional Customs spokesperson said.

He was immediately arrested on suspicion of violating Japan's Narcotics and Psychotropics Control Act. Local customs officials filed a criminal accusation with the regional prosecutor's office on Thursday to begin formal charging proceedings against Harris.

An official with a police station in Tomigusuku city in southern Okinawa confirmed that Harris remains in custody. The official declined to comment on whether Harris has admitted to the allegations.

Harris' representatives did not immediately respond to requests for comment, and it was unclear whether he has retained legal representation in Japan.

Harris had arrived in Okinawa for sightseeing purposes, travelling from Britain via Taiwan, where he had a flight layover, the customs spokesperson said. Okinawa is a popular resort destination some 1,500 km (930 miles) southwest of Tokyo, known for its beaches and American military bases.

Japan maintains strict drug laws with severe penalties for possession and smuggling offences. The country has some of the toughest narcotics regulations among developed nations, with even small amounts of illegal substances resulting in criminal prosecution. Punishments, however, are not as harsh as in other Asian countries such as China and Singapore, where drug offenders can face the death penalty.

Foreigners arrested in recent years in Japan for suspected drug offences include former Australian rugby league player Blake Ferguson in December 2021. In 1980, former Beatle Paul McCartney was arrested after arriving in Tokyo with a bag of marijuana and was banned from entering Japan for years.

data.title: Chevron-operated Gorgon project secures $2 billion investment nod
data.description:
The Australian unit of U.S. oil giant Chevron Corp said on Friday that the partners of the Gorgon Joint Venture have made a final investment decision on the A$3 billion ($1.98 billion) Gorgon Stage 3 development off Western Australia's northwest coast.

The investment will link the offshore Geryon and Eurytion natural gas fields to Gorgon's existing infrastructure on Barrow Island.

The Chevron-operated Gorgon Project is a joint venture among the Australian units of Chevron, Exxon Mobil , and Shell , which collectively own roughly 97.3%, with Japan's Osaka Gas and JERA and U.S.-based firm MidOcean holding the remaining stake.

The development would maintain production at Gorgon, enabling the long-term supply of domestic gas for Western Australia households and industry, and liquefied natural gas for international customers in Asia, Chevron Australia president Balaji Krishnamurthy said.

Gorgon has the capacity to produce 300 terajoules per day of gas for the Western Australia market and 15.6 million tonnes of LNG per year, said Chevron Australia.

($1 = 1.5135 Australian dollars)

data.title: Japanese Shares Fall on Market Caution
data.description:
The Nikkei 225 Index dropped 1.2% to below 50,500, while the broader Topix Index fell 1% to 3,365 on Friday, trimming gains from the previous session as investors grew cautious ahead of a key US inflation report that could influence Federal Reserve policy.

US stocks stalled overnight after jobs data showed resilience in the US labor market, slightly reducing expectations for a Fed rate cut next week.

Rising global bond yields also pressured equities, with the Bank of Japan signaling a potential rate hike later this month.

Notable losses came from heavyweight firms including Advantest (-2.5%), Fanuc (-0.9%), Fujikura (-0.3%), Tokyo Electron (-2.3%) and Toyota Motor (-2%).

In corporate news, Mitsubishi UFJ announced plans to partner with Morgan Stanley in asset management, building on 17 years of collaboration.

data.title: As Nashville's Population Booms, a Wealthy Suburb Clings to Its Small-Town Feel — WSJ
data.description:
By Daisy Prince | Video and photography by William DeShazer for WSJ

When Sarah Reisner's father died two years ago, the resident of Belle Meade, a lush suburb about 15 minutes from Nashville's downtown, wrote heartfelt notes to many of her father's friends to thank them for coming to the funeral and bringing food. She left them in her mailbox for the postal carrier to collect.

A Kentucky-based gang of thieves who had been stealing checks from Belle Meade mailboxes grabbed the letters. Realizing the notes were worthless, they simply threw them in a ditch along Belle Meade Boulevard. A neighbor walking her dog spotted the discarded letters, dusted them off, and returned the notes to her.

"And this is just an example of how neighbors watch out for each other here. It's a community," said Reisner.

The thieves were eventually caught by Belle Meade's dedicated criminal investigator, Thomas Sexton, using footage of the perpetrators' license plates captured by security cameras mounted on street signs throughout Belle Meade.

Belle Meade locals are proud of their area's small-town feel. While they pride themselves on being friendly — every new neighbor is greeted with a casserole or an invitation to the local "Friday Lunches" where Sexton, singer John Hiatt, Fortune 500 CEOs and the neighborhood plumbers might meet up for a Mexican meal — they still love keeping certain things just the way they are.

"The residents of Belle Meade like our green spaces," says Reisner, a fourth-generation Nashvillian. "We like having nice big yards where we can have our dogs. We don't want the 'tall and skinnys,' " the nickname for the large McMansions that have sprung up on tiny lots in nearby neighborhoods such as Forest Hills and Oak Hills. "We don't want increased traffic that prevents people from walking around. It just feels safer when it's not congested."

During the pandemic, the city of Nashville grew by 6.4% between 2020 and 2024, according to data from the U.S. Census Bureau's Population Estimate program. The growth came on the heels of a flurry of corporate relocations to the Nashville area.

AllianceBernstein brought 1,250 employees from New York in 2018. Amazon followed in 2018 by committing to creating an additional 5,000 jobs in the area. More are on the way. Oracle announced in April 2024 that it will relocate its corporate offices from Austin, a move they expect to complete in 2030. The new location will employ about 8,500 people.

While downtown Nashville has borne the brunt of the population growth, for the city's wealthiest old guard, and many of its new moneyed transplants, the tony enclave of Belle Meade is the place to be. It is the richest city in Tennessee with a total population of 2,901 as of 2020, and a median annual income of more than $250,000, according to the 2023 American Community Survey by the U.S. Census Bureau.

"I think they are steeped in tradition in Belle Meade," says real-estate agent Barbara Keith Payne, from Pilkerton Realtors. "That's part of the attraction to outsiders and insiders."

Private banker Sarah Pickens, 41, who lives in New York, recently bought a roughly $3 million house on a 1-acre lot in Belle Meade with her husband, Kyle Bransfield, and found it to be incredibly welcoming. "We didn't know anybody when we moved in, and we were just gobbled up by our neighbors," says Pickens. "My husband had knee surgery last year, and I was brought meals for five days. That does not happen in New York."

Because Pickens and her husband don't have children (they have two Cavalier King Charles Spaniels), she says they are in hot demand with the empty-nester crowd who know they are available to grab a glass of wine on a Monday instead of sitting on the sidelines watching a soccer game.

"Belle Meade was supposed to be a place of rest," Pickens laughs. "We have these crazy jobs, and my social calendar is packed when I go to Nashville — coffee hour, lunch, cocktail parties — even more than in New York."

Pickens, whose family originally comes from South Carolina and who grew up attending church regularly, appreciates that on Sundays, people switch their phones off. "Sometimes I'll try to text the handyman on Sunday and get a reply saying, 'We don't respond until Monday morning.' Everybody's at the country club on Sunday for brunch after church, and everybody's dressed up."

Although small, with just 1,150 homes on 3.1 square miles, Belle Meade has its own police force that will do house checks for free when residents are out of town. The city has a trash service that collects trash cans from behind homes so owners don't have to leave them out on the street, and a weekly wood chipper service that disposes of the leaves, twigs and fallen branches produced by Belle Meade's collection of ancient trees. The services are funded by property taxes paid to the city of Belle Meade, which has its own city charter separate from metro Nashville. Residents also pay property tax to Davidson County for fire, ambulance and other municipal services not provided by Belle Meade.

Known as the Belle Meade Bubble — residents rarely need to leave — the neighborhood has long been the epicenter of Nashville's oldest, most elite families. Many of the oldest homes in Belle Meade were originally Georgian or Greek Revival and built in the late 1920s before the Great Depression. Most sat on lots of an acre or more. There is a historic overlay committee that prevents any buildings of architectural importance from being torn down. Belle Meade's zoning codes and its board of commissioners enforce strict building rules.

Residents have included Jack Massey, co-founder of the Hospital Corporation of America, who took several companies public. Older families in the area include book distribution and shipping entrepreneur John R. Ingram and family, as well as the late founder of Maxwell House, Joel Owlsey Cheek. Vice president Al Gore lived in Belle Meade. Star musicians Amy Grant and Vince Gill are current residents. Reese Witherspoon also had a house in Belle Meade.

Dr. Thomas F. Frist Jr., a billionaire philanthropist who co-founded HCA with Massey, sold his Belle Meade estate following the death of his wife, setting a city record of $32 million in 2024.

At the neighborhood's historic heart are two properties: Cheekwood Botanical Gardens & Estate, an imposing gray mansion with columns set on 55 acres that has long been a cornerstone of Nashville society. Once a private house completed in 1932 by the Cheek family, and inspired by the historic English houses of the 18th century, the home is now an art and botanical garden museum. Until recently, it was the location of the annual white-tie Swan Ball, a fundraiser for the museum and the pinnacle of Nashville's social calendar.

The other notable landmark is the Belle Meade Country Club, originally a plantation turned stud farm. It has an 18-hole championship golf course, 10 outdoor tennis courts, eight indoor tennis courts, a spa/fitness center and swimming pool. At the formal Iroquois Room, jackets are required for men after 6 p.m. Politeness to other members and staff is so paramount that the club has a statement about club decorum on its home page.

Like many exclusive clubs, Belle Meade Country Club has a high bar for entry. Memberships are mostly multigenerational, and applicants are required to reside in Nashville for at least two years before they can apply, a policy that was in place long before the recent influx.

Once the province of Nashville's elite, the club is now the hottest ticket in town among newly transplanted corporate executives and music entrepreneurs.

But like Nashville's congested downtown, the club's infrastructure wasn't designed to accept such a large number of newcomers. Longtime members lament the changes: The pool area is overcrowded, tennis courts require advance booking, and worst of all, you can no longer just stroll onto the golf course.

Established club members were reluctant to go on the record about the changes brought by newcomers, and prefer to quietly gripe about them while sipping the club's signature drink, the Ed Special (Kahlua and cream). Plans are afoot to renovate the pool complex but there is no room to expand the golf course and the tennis courts.

The premium placed on a Belle Meade address has transformed the neighborhood's landscape. During the pandemic, transplants from California and New York, flush with cash from selling their coastal homes at top dollar, found themselves competing for Belle Meade's limited real estate. Belle Meade asking prices have crested $14 million, but none so far has come close to the price paid for the Frist mansion.

The area's popularity has driven the median sale price for all homes sales to $1.386 million in September 2025, according to Realtor.com. The median sale price in September 2020 was $799,000. ( News Corp, owner of The Wall Street Journal, also operates Realtor.com under license from the National Association of Realtors.)

Changes don't sit easy in such an entrenched place. A recent plan to add sidewalks to historic Belle Meade Boulevard, a roadway with a wide grass median, was recently struck down after a group of Belle Meade neighbors argued that it would detract from the area's beauty.

Local Lynne Warne bought her Belle Meade home in 2006 for $1.98 million and has put in $1.2 million. "We like our neighborhood just as it is," she says. "We don't want it to change, unless it's undeniably for the better. The parklike setting of Belle Meade is a major draw. Adding a sidewalk through the middle of Belle Meade Boulevard and along our streets would mean removal of many large established trees."

Newcomers don't always understand the locals' wish to keep the neighborhood looking a certain way.

data.title: Florida lawmakers urge Trump to reject drilling off state's western coast
data.description:
Florida's entire U.S. congressional delegation on Thursday urged President Donald Trump to leave their state out of a plan by his administration to expand oil and gas drilling to new coastal areas, saying it would threaten the state's military and tourism industries.

WHY IT'S IMPORTANT

The letter to Trump from Florida Senators Rick Scott and Ashley Moody and the state's 28 members of the House of Representatives was a rare bipartisan pushback against the Republican president's policies. Most of Florida's members of Congress are Republicans.

KEY QUOTE

"The risks posed by new offshore drilling far outweigh any short-term gains," the lawmakers said in their letter.

BY THE NUMBERS

More than 50,000 jobs in the Florida Panhandle are tied to operations at military facilities connected to the Gulf Test Range, a vast area over the Gulf of Mexico where advanced weapons and air combat tactics are tested, the letter said, noting that drilling would result in encroachment of the Gulf Test Range.

Florida beaches generate $127.7 billion a year in tourism spending and support 2.1 million jobs.

CONTEXT

The Interior Department last month unveiled a proposal that opened the door to future leasing in the eastern portion of the Gulf of Mexico, which has long been protected from energy development because it is used for military testing and training. Florida officials, including Republican Governor Ron DeSantis, have opposed changes to that policy.

Trump's Florida residence, Mar-a-Lago, is on the eastern side of the state.

THE REPLY

The White House did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

data.title: Meta Plans to Shift Spending Away From the Metaverse — WSJ
data.description:
By Meghan Bobrowsky and Georgia Wells

Meta Platforms is planning cuts to the metaverse, an arena Chief Executive Officer Mark Zuckerberg once called the future of the company.

The proposed changes are part of Meta's annual budget planning for 2026, and the company plans to shift spending from the metaverse to artificial-intelligence wearables, according to a person familiar with the matter. Several tech companies including Apple are working on wearable devices they believe might become the next major computing platform.

The decision marks a sharp departure from the vision Zuckerberg laid out in 2021, when he changed the name of his company to Meta Platforms, from Facebook, to reflect his belief in growth opportunities in the online digital realm known as the metaverse. Meta has seen operating losses of more than $77 billion since 2020 in its Reality Labs division, which includes its metaverse work.

On Thursday, investors cheered Meta's decision, reflecting concern many have voiced about the direction of the money-losing bet over the years. Shares jumped more than 3%.

While Zuckerberg has regularly asked executives to trim their budgets in recent years, he is focusing on the metaverse group now because the immersive technology hasn't gained the traction the company had anticipated, according to the person familiar with the matter.

While most of Zuckerberg's public remarks for the past year have been about AI, he has insisted a few times that the metaverse bet could yet pay off. In January, he told investors that 2025 would be a "pivotal" year for the metaverse.

"This is the year when a number of the long-term investments that we've been working on that will make the metaverse more visually stunning and inspiring will really start to land," he said.

Meta's plan to reduce its metaverse budget was earlier reported by Bloomberg.

Early on, Meta's bet-the-company move on the metaverse hit rough patches. About a year after the rebrand, internal company documents showed the transition grappling with glitchy technology, uninterested users and a lack of clarity about what it would take to succeed. At the time, Zuckerberg said the transition to a more-immersive online experience would take years.

Meanwhile, artificial intelligence has emerged as the primary focus of where the broader tech industry sees the future. Tech executives believe AI will reshape how consumers interact with tech as well as how the industry makes money.

Meta is now giving priority to investments in AI, including its AI glasses. In June, Zuckerberg announced the creation of a Superintelligence division to recognize the effort formally.

He paid special attention to researcher recruiting, to reflect the new primacy of AI. He offered $100 million pay packages to AI specialists to lure them to join his Superintelligence division and hired around 50 people.

The company's Ray-Ban AI glasses have gained momentum in recent years. Meta's hardware partner, EssilorLuxottica, said on a call this year that it had sold more than two million pairs and expected to expand production capacity to 10 million pairs annually by the end of 2026.

Investors are closely watching Meta's AI transformation. To streamline its AI division, in October, Meta announced internally that the company would cut about 600 jobs in its AI division. The cuts were aimed at the company's teams focused on long-term AI research and other initiatives, and not the new team that houses Zuckerberg's multimillion-dollar hires. Weeks later, Meta shares fell after the company warned of "aggressive" capital expenditure growth to stay competitive in the AI arms race.

Write to Meghan Bobrowsky at meghan.bobrowsky@wsj.com and Georgia Wells at georgia.wells@wsj.com

data.title: Dollar hovers near five-week low on Fed rate cut bets
data.description:
By Kevin Buckland

The U.S. dollar languished not far from a five-week low against its major peers on Friday as investors braced for a Federal Reserve rate cut next week.

Markets widely expect a quarter point reduction when the policy-setting Federal Open Market Committee meets on December 9-10, and a focus will be on any signals about how much additional easing lies ahead.

The dollar index , which measures the currency against six rivals, was flat at 99.065 early in Asia. A small gain overnight snapped a nine-day losing streak, but the index had dipped to a five-week low of 98.765 earlier that session, and it remains on course for a 0.4% decline this week.

Traders are pricing around 86% odds of Fed cut next Wednesday, and potentially 2-3 more reductions next year, LSEG data showed.

Fed officials have been carefully watching the labour market to determine whether the economy needs further support.

Data overnight showed the number of Americans filing new applications for unemployment benefits fell to a more than three-year low last week, but may have been skewed by the Thanksgiving holiday.

The data picture remains incomplete following the record-long government shutdown delayed some releases and prevented other data from ever being collected.

Crucial monthly payrolls figures would ordinarily be published later on Friday, but have been delayed, and the previous month's numbers were never released.

However, one of the Fed's preferred inflation gauges - the PCE deflator - will be published later on Friday, although the data is for September. Economists surveyed by LSEG expect a 0.2% monthly increase in the core number.

"An increase of 0.2% per month or below will encourage the FOMC to cut the Funds rate next week in our view," Commonwealth Bank of Australia currency strategist Carol Kong wrote in a client note.

"Our analysis suggests the risk is a soft increase in core PCE inflation of only 0.1%."

The dollar was little changed at 155.18 yen .

The euro was flat at $1.1647 and sterling held steady at $1.3326 after easing back from Wednesday's six-week peak in the previous session.

The dollar has come under additional pressure in recent days with investors also weighing the prospect of White House economic adviser Kevin Hassett taking over as Fed Chair after Jerome Powell’s term ends in May. Hassett is expected to push for more rate cuts.

Next week sees a parade of central bank policy decisions, with the Reserve Bank of Australia's coming on Tuesday, the Bank of Canada's on Wednesday and the Swiss National Bank's on Thursday.

That continues the following week with the European Central Bank, the Bank of England, Sweden's Riksbank, and the Bank of Japan setting policy.

Three government officials told Reuters that the BOJ is likely to raise rates this month, although what comes after remains uncertain, with markets only fully pricing in one more rate hike next year and around a 50% chance of another.

The Aussie was stable at $0.6609 after jumping to a two-month high of $0.6624 on Thursday.

Canada's loonie was little changed at C$1.3961 per greenback, while the Swiss franc was steady at 0.8035 per dollar after dropping back sharply from Wednesday's two-week high of 0.7992 in the overnight session.
